description_long: This object consists of multiple rectangular traffic signs arranged in four rows, each supported by two vertical posts. The signs are white with a red border and display the names of various Spanish cities and towns, such as LLEIDA, BARCELONA, GIRONA, and TARRAGONA, in bold black uppercase letters. The signs are made of a flat, likely metal or composite panel, with the text and border printed or painted on the surface. The posts appear to be metallic and have a dark gray, slightly textured finish. The signs are designed to be highly visible and legible from a distance, suitable for marking city or town entrances. There are no symbols or pictograms, only text.
description: A group of rectangular, post-mounted traffic signs with red borders and black text displaying Spanish city names, supported by dark gray metal posts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the text on the signs, with city names clearly visible and the arrangement of four rows.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the signs and the alignment of the posts in rows.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the backs of the signs and the supporting posts, with no visible text.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thinness and alignment of the signs.
